skill.name: expo-deployment
skill.description: Provides deployment guidance for Expo apps, including production builds, app store submission, OTA updates, and release management.
skill.summary: Plan and manage Expo production releases across builds, stores, OTA updates, and rollout channels.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported hardcoded URLs and weak cryptographic algorithm matches in SKILL.md. Review found the URLs are documentation/source links, and the weak cryptography matches are ordinary text substrings, with no executable code, cryptographic use, data exfiltration, or prompt injection evidence.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Documentation URL References
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged hardcoded URLs at SKILL.md lines 4 and 72. These are source repository references in metadata and resources, not runtime network requests or data transmission.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines contain GitHub source links only. No executable network call, request body, credentials, or external command is present in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Match
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptographic algorithm matches at SKILL.md lines 3 and 27. The matches occur inside ordinary prose and metadata, with no cryptographic API, key handling, hashing, or encryption logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is not code and does not invoke DES, MD5, SHA1, or any cryptographic primitive. The surrounding context is deployment guidance only.

content.value_statement: Expo releases require coordinated build settings, store preparation, OTA channels, and rollout checks. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a focused workflow for planning safer production deployments.
actual_capabilities: Guides production build configuration for Expo applications.
Outlines iOS App Store and Google Play submission preparation.
Explains OTA update channel planning and rollout management.
Provides pre-deployment checks for versions, tests, environment variables, and bundle size.
Covers release management practices, staged rollouts, monitoring, and rollback planning.
limitations: Does not run Expo CLI, EAS Build, or EAS Submit commands by itself.
Does not manage signing credentials, store accounts, or production secrets.
Does not replace current Expo, Apple, or Google policy documentation.
Does not guarantee app store approval or prevent platform review issues.

use_cases.title: Prepare a First Production Release
use_cases.description: Create a deployment checklist before submitting a new Expo app to app stores.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Staged Mobile Rollouts
use_cases.description: Plan production and staging channels for OTA updates and controlled release adoption.

use_cases.title: Review Release Readiness
use_cases.description: Check versioning, environment settings, metadata, testing, monitoring, and rollback readiness before launch.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Deployment Checklist
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a production deployment checklist for my Expo app. Include build settings, testing, versioning, store metadata, OTA updates, monitoring, and rollback steps.

prompt_templates.title: Review App Store Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Expo app release plan for iOS App Store and Google Play submission. Identify missing metadata, build configuration, testing, screenshots, privacy, and review requirements.

prompt_templates.title: Plan OTA Update Channels
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an OTA update strategy for my Expo app with staging, production, phased rollout, monitoring, and rollback procedures. Explain the release channel choices.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Complex Release
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this Expo production release process for operational risk. Focus on credentials, environment variables, versioning, EAS build profiles, app review, monitoring, incident response, and rollback control.

output_examples.input: I need to ship my first Expo app to both app stores.
output: A release checklist grouped by build setup, store metadata, testing, privacy review, submission, and post-launch monitoring.
A short list of blocking items to resolve before creating production binaries.

output_examples.input: I want OTA updates for staging and production users.
output: A channel plan that separates staging validation from production rollout.
A rollback and monitoring process for detecting failed updates and limiting user impact.

output_examples.input: Review this release process before launch week.
output: A readiness assessment covering versioning, environment variables, build profiles, app store tasks, analytics, error tracking, and support ownership.
Prioritized recommendations for launch blockers and follow-up improvements.

best_practices: Keep separate staging and production release channels with clear promotion rules.
Test production builds on real devices before submitting or publishing updates.
Prepare monitoring, analytics, and rollback steps before each release window.
anti_patterns: Publishing OTA updates without testing them against the current production binary.
Changing production environment variables during release without a rollback plan.
Submitting to app stores before screenshots, metadata, privacy details, and version numbers are final.

faq.question: Can this skill deploy my Expo app automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides deployment guidance and checklists, but it does not execute Expo, EAS, Apple, or Google commands.

faq.question: Does it support both iOS and Android releases?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance covers App Store Connect, Google Play Console, production builds, and shared release planning.

faq.question: Can it help with OTA updates?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers update channels, staged rollout planning, monitoring, adoption checks, and rollback preparation.

faq.question: Does it manage signing credentials?
faq.answer: No. You must manage credentials, certificates, keystores, and store account access through approved Expo and platform tools.

faq.question: Should I still read Expo documentation?
faq.answer: Yes. Use this skill as workflow guidance and verify commands, policies, and platform requirements against current official documentation.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found only documentation links and false-positive text matches, with no executable code or confirmed security issue.
